context: Tests whether the agent adds a commit body explaining 'why' for complex changes and includes issue number references when a tracked issue is associated with the change. Simple changes should remain concise without an unnecessary body.

checklist.name: Body on Redis migration
checklist.description: The session token Redis migration commit (Change 2) includes a body section (text below the subject line separated by a blank line)

checklist.name: Body explains why (Redis)
checklist.description: The body of the Redis migration commit explains the reason for the change (e.g., multi-instance session issue, random logouts, load balancing) rather than just restating what changed

checklist.name: Issue ref on Redis migration
checklist.description: The Redis migration commit (Change 2) references issue #88 somewhere in the message

checklist.name: Body on image pipeline rewrite
checklist.description: The image resizing pipeline rewrite commit (Change 4) includes a body section (text below the subject line separated by a blank line)

checklist.name: Body explains why (image pipeline)
checklist.description: The body of the image pipeline commit explains the reason for the change (e.g., request timeouts, event loop blocking, degraded performance) rather than just restating what changed

checklist.name: Issue ref on image pipeline
checklist.description: The image pipeline rewrite commit (Change 4) references issue #102 somewhere in the message

checklist.name: No spurious body on simple commits
checklist.description: The straightforward commits (Changes 1, 3, and 5) do NOT include a multi-line body — the subject line alone is sufficient

checklist.name: Issue ref format
checklist.description: Issue references use the '#' prefix format (e.g., '#88', '#102', 'Closes #88') rather than plain numbers or URLs

checklist.name: Imperative mood throughout
checklist.description: All five commit subject lines use imperative mood verbs (e.g., 'add', 'migrate', 'rewrite') rather than past tense or gerunds
